name: perceived_ethnic_discrimination
description: Measures the degree to which respondents perceive that indigenous people face discrimination and unequal treatment in employment, rights assertion, and daily life in Mexico.

name: indigenous_rights_and_autonomy
description: Captures attitudes toward indigenous peoples' political self-determination, land rights, linguistic access to public services, and respect for their human rights by the state.

name: social_distance_toward_indigenous_people
description: Measures the willingness of non-indigenous respondents to engage in close personal, domestic, or professional relationships with indigenous people, reflecting affective prejudice and social exclusion.

name: attributions_for_indigenous_disadvantage
description: Assesses whether respondents attribute indigenous poverty and hardship to structural/systemic factors or to individual and cultural deficiencies, capturing meritocratic versus structural explanatory frames.

name: indigenous_cultural_knowledge_and_identity
description: Measures respondents' factual knowledge about indigenous groups and language speakers in Mexico, their own ethnic self-identification, and family connections to indigenous language heritage.
